scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 2025

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell regarding the residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft (hereinafter referred to as the "Schedule Property"). A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

6. The total consideration agreed upon for the sale of the Schedule Property is Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs only).

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakhs only) to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 itself.

8. The said advance payment was made via RTGS bearing UTR Number HDFC20240110987654. A copy of the bank receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.

9. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- (Rupees Sixty Lakhs only) was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

10. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the obligations under the Agreement to Sell, including the payment of the balance consideration within the stipulated time.

11. On 05.06.2024, prior to the deadline for payment of the balance consideration, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the Sale Deed. The Defendant stated in the said letter that he intended to sell the Schedule Property to another person who had made a higher offer. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as **Annexure D**.

12. Upon receipt of the Defendant's refusal, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to specifically perform the contract and execute the Sale Deed as per the Agreement to Sell. A copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.

13. The Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice and has consistently refused to execute the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff, thereby committing breach of the Agreement to Sell.

DAMAGES

14. Due to the Defendant's refusal to execute the Sale Deed, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the possession and ownership of the Schedule Property despite having paid a substantial advance of Rs. 25,00,000/-.

15. The Plaintiff has kept the balance amount of Rs. 60,00,000/- ready for payment, but the Defendant's breach has prevented the completion of the transaction, causing financial prejudice to the Plaintiff.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The present cause is governed by Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment — court SHALL grant unless S.11(2), S.14, or S.16 exception), Section 16, Section 21, Section 22.

17. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

18. The Plaintiff also relies on Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

19. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

20. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

21. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

23. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, directing the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 21 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

CONSISTENCY GATE — FORBIDDEN CONTENT

The civil drafting pipeline stopped before finalization for the following reasons:
1. Draft claims 'liquidated damages s74' as relief — this is a defence concept, not a plaintiff claim.
2. Draft claims 'mitigation credit' as relief — this is a defence concept, not a plaintiff claim.

Clarify the above issues and rerun drafting.
